plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% and QQQ to 20% for stable income and broad index exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to manage single-name concentration.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation and stable growth.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for approximately 1.5% monthly growth by prioritizing capital preservation and stability. It significantly reduces risk by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, but also limits upside potential. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL completely as it is flagged and leveraged.
Reduce SPAL to 2% to lower overall leveraged ETF exposure.
Buy TSLA for 8% of the book, leveraging a strong scanner setup.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a high-scoring software name.
Trim SMH to 10% to manage semiconductor concentration.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% monthly growth by blending broad index, quality tech, and reduced leverage. It aims for risk-first growth, maintaining a moderate cash buffer. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns compared to conservative.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL completely to reallocate capital to stronger setups.
Reduce SPAL to 2% to free up capital for higher conviction plays.
Buy TSLA for 12% of the book, aggressively leaning into a top scanner setup.
Increase CRWD to 18% to maximize exposure to a high-scoring software name.
Buy NVDU for 10%, adding another high-scoring scanner setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for around 4% monthly growth by stretching into high-scoring setups and using leverage. This strategy significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown risk, and leveraged ETFs are subject to decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the higher volatility and potential for rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Significantly increase QQQI to 35% to prioritize monthly distributions.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged exposure and reduce beta.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce high-beta single-name exposure.
Reduce SMH to 5% to lower high-beta sector concentration.
This plan focuses on generating steady income with lower drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% monthly yield by rotating into income-focused ETFs and broad index funds. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall. This is for cash-like income, not a savings account.
